title: Flaky, Lightly Scrambled Egyptian Emmentaler Cheese Hash
description: A light, one-pot Egyptian dish featuring Emmentaler cheese, whisk, egg-free-friendly, vessel=lazy-susan-spinner.

ingredients.name: Emmentaler cheese, shredded
ingredients.notes: Imported or domestic, whichever is available.

ingredients.name: Breadcrumbs
ingredients.notes: Panko or regular, whichever is available.

ingredients.name: Garlic, minced
ingredients.notes: Mince until well combined with butter.

ingredients.name: Chopped parsley
ingredients.notes: Chopped from fresh parsley for garnish.

ingredients.name: Black pepper
ingredients.notes: Adjust black pepper to your preference.

ingredients.name: Water
ingredients.notes: Use to prevent cheese from sticking to pan.

instructions: Add butter to the lazy Susan-spinner and melt over medium heat.
Add minced garlic and cook for 1 minute until well combined with butter.
Add shredded Emmentaler cheese to the pan and stir until it begins to melt.
Add breadcrumbs to the pan and stir until well combined with melted cheese.
Add salt and black pepper to the pan and stir until well combined.
Drizzle water into the pan to prevent cheese from sticking.
Use a whisk to break up any clumps of cheese and create a light, flaky texture.
Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes until cheese is lightly scrambled.
Transfer the cheesy hash to a serving platter and garnish with chopped parsley.
